text: In this instance you are wanting to pass some options to `browserify`. Using a streaming interface for this is not how you want to do it; you just want the options at runtime. text: I agree that in a very specific cases one might need less comparisons. For examples, if all the N elements in an array are equal, you will need at most N-1 comparisons. But in general case (when you assume all possible permutations of array are valid) it is incorrect. Assuming all elements in array are unique you have N! possible permutations. Each comparison reduces yields at most 2 answers with new information (bigger/smaller) so each comparison reduces the amount of permutation by at most 2. The amount of comparisons needed is ~log(N!).

text: You don't use the DFS algorithm to "build" a graph, you use it on an existing graph. I'm not quite sure what your problem is but let me put it this way.
